subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **Gem tokens** — 25 tokens in 5 colors + Gold + Pearls
- **Game board** (5×5 token grid)
- **Noble cards** (4 face-up)
- **Jewel cards** — 3 levels (rows of 3-4 face-up)
- **Royal cards** — Special bonuses
- **Token bag**

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place 5×5 board** in center.
2. **Fill board** — Draw 25 tokens from bag and place on the grid.
3. **Lay out jewel cards** — 3 rows (Level 1, 2, 3) with face-up cards.
4. **Place noble cards** face up.
5. **Place Royal cards** nearby.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **No starting tokens or cards.**
- **Determine first player** — Youngest or random.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Splendor: Duel is a 2-player gem-collecting engine builder. Take gems from a shared grid, buy jewel cards that give permanent bonuses and prestige. Three different win conditions add tension.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, do ONE of:
1. **Take gems** — Select up to 3 gems from the board using specific patterns.
2. **Reserve a card** — Take a jewel card into your hand (max 3 reserved) and take 1 gold token.
3. **Purchase a card** — Buy a face-up or reserved card by spending gems.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Taking Gems
- Take gems in a straight line (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal) from the 5×5 board.
- **1 gem** — Any single gem.
- **2 gems** — Must be adjacent in a line and different colors.
- **3 gems** — Must be in a straight line; triggers a privilege.
- **Pearl tokens** — Special tokens needed for certain cards.
- **Gold tokens** — Wild; can substitute for any color.

#### Purchasing Cards
- Pay the gem cost shown on the card.
- The card gives you a permanent gem bonus (reduces future costs).
- Some cards have crowns (used for noble attraction and win conditions).
- **Privileges** — Gained from taking 3+ gems or special actions; spend to take an extra gem.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Token limit** — Max 10 tokens per player.
- **Board replenish** — After taking gems, refill from the bag.
- **Nobles** — When you meet a noble's requirements, claim them for prestige.
- **Scrolls** — Some cards have special powers (take extra gems, replay turn, etc.).

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Three Win Conditions (any one wins immediately)
1. **20 Prestige Points** — Accumulate 20+ prestige from cards and nobles.
2. **10 Crowns** — Collect 10+ crowns from card symbols.
3. **Color Monopoly** — Collect 10+ points in cards of a single gem color.

Game ends immediately when any condition is met on a player's turn.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Multiple win paths** — Watch for all 3 win conditions; don't tunnel on one.
- **Engine building** — Early cards should reduce costs for expensive later cards.
- **Gems from grid** — Plan your gem selection carefully; the grid pattern matters.
- **Watch opponent** — Block their win conditions while pursuing your own.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Crown rush** — Collecting crown cards can surprise an opponent with a quick 10-crown win.
- **Color stacking** — Focus on one color for the monopoly victory path.
- **Privilege timing** — Privileges give tempo advantage; use them efficiently.
- **Denial** — Take gems your opponent needs from the grid.
